column1: # Talo false-friend screen — forms to AVOID minting because they are a common,
# different-meaning (or rude) word in a major language (docs/0003 §7b; the mirror
# of the obscenity blocklist). The collision checker BLOCKS forms listed at
# SEVERE/HIGH by default; MEDIUM/LOW are documentation, surfaced only with a
# tightened --ff-block. Matching is on the WHOLE form (exact), so an entry warns
# against minting that exact root — it does not flag embeddings (that is the
# CURATION: drawn from the largest L1s, prioritising (a) very high-frequency
# everyday words (pronouns, "rice", "face") in big languages, since an accidental
# clash there is most jarring, and (b) rude/taboo exact forms. Every SEVERE/HIGH
# entry was checked NOT to be a current lexicon root (the screen stays exit 0 over
# data/lexicon.tsv). Broad but not exhaustive; a native-speaker pass per major L1
# should still review it before a v1.0 freeze.
# saya/suka are ALREADY minted roots, so they are recorded at MEDIUM (documented,
# non-blocking) rather than HIGH — re-minting an existing root is a freeze-time call:

column3: I / me in Indonesian (Talo saya = wing, ANI-010)
